name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 11.08.2025

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 11.08.2025.pdf

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Kharif Sowing Status: As on 08 August, 2025, the area sown under Kharif crops is 995.63 lakh ha (including 57.31 lakh ha (109% of normal area) in Sugarcane) as compared to 957.15 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year. About 90.1 percent of the normal area has been sown  under  the  Kharif  season  crops.  The  status  of  sowing  under  Rice,  Pulses  and  Coarse Cereals is better than in the same period of last year.
texts.text: Kharif Sowing Status: As on 08 August, 2025, the area sown under Kharif crops is 995.63 lakh ha (including 57.31 lakh ha (109% of normal area) in Sugarcane) as compared to 957.15 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year. About 90.1 percent of the normal area has been sown  under  the  Kharif  season  crops.  The  status  of  sowing  under  Rice,  Pulses  and  Coarse Cereals is better than in the same period of last year.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update :
texts.text: 2. Weather Update :

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature: Maximum  temperatures is  likely  to  be above  normal  in  Central  India, Gujarat, Madhya Maharashtra, Karnataka and Rajasthan.
texts.text: Temperature: Maximum  temperatures is  likely  to  be above  normal  in  Central  India, Gujarat, Madhya Maharashtra, Karnataka and Rajasthan.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall  Distribution: From  June  01-  Aug  10 2025,  the  All-India  cumulative rainfall is  1% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India:(-)17%; (ii) Northwest India: 16%; (iii) Central India: 5%; and South Peninsula:(-)2%.
texts.text: Rainfall  Distribution: From  June  01-  Aug  10 2025,  the  All-India  cumulative rainfall is  1% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India:(-)17%; (ii) Northwest India: 16%; (iii) Central India: 5%; and South Peninsula:(-)2%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated  heavy  to  very  heavy  rainfall  likely  over  Uttarakhand  and  Himachal Pradesh during next 7 days with isolated extremely heavy (≥21cm) over Uttarakhand on 13 th  August, 2025.
texts.text:  Isolated  heavy  to  very  heavy  rainfall  likely  over  Uttarakhand  and  Himachal Pradesh during next 7 days with isolated extremely heavy (≥21cm) over Uttarakhand on 13 th  August, 2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Increase in rainfall activity over east central India and adjoining north peninsular India;  with  a  fresh  spell  of  heavy  to  very  heavy  rainfall  over  Madhya  Pradesh, Vidarbha,  Odisha,  Chhattisgarh,  Coastal  Andhra  Pradesh,  and  Telangana  during 13 th  to 16 th  August, 2025.
texts.text:  Increase in rainfall activity over east central India and adjoining north peninsular India;  with  a  fresh  spell  of  heavy  to  very  heavy  rainfall  over  Madhya  Pradesh, Vidarbha,  Odisha,  Chhattisgarh,  Coastal  Andhra  Pradesh,  and  Telangana  during 13 th  to 16 th  August, 2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  A low-pressure area is likely to form over northwest & adjoining west central Bay of Bengal around 13 th  August, 2025.
texts.text:  A low-pressure area is likely to form over northwest & adjoining west central Bay of Bengal around 13 th  August, 2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The  Western  Disturbance  seen  as  a  cyclonic  circulation  over  north  Pakistan  & neighborhood in lower & middle tropospheric levels.
texts.text:  The  Western  Disturbance  seen  as  a  cyclonic  circulation  over  north  Pakistan  & neighborhood in lower & middle tropospheric levels.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: District-wise rainfall distribution of States is attached in Annexure I.
texts.text: District-wise rainfall distribution of States is attached in Annexure I.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  7 th   August  2025,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  161 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is  132.40 Billion Cubic Meter, which is 72.5% of total live storage capacity (182.496 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 114.5 percent of last year's storage and 133.1 percent of the average storage  of  the  last  ten  years  during  the  corresponding  period.  The  overall  storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  7 th   August  2025,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  161 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is  132.40 Billion Cubic Meter, which is 72.5% of total live storage capacity (182.496 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 114.5 percent of last year's storage and 133.1 percent of the average storage  of  the  last  ten  years  during  the  corresponding  period.  The  overall  storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
texts.text: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater Levels :  About 43.1% of the total monitoring wells show depth to water level in the range of 0 to 5 m below ground level and 37.6% show depth to water level in the range of 5 to 10 m below ground level. About 19.2% of the total monitoring wells show water  level  deeper  than  10  m  below  ground  level.  The  groundwater  level  fluctuation analysis  of  Pre-monsoon  2025  with decadal mean  of  Pre-monsoon (2015-2024) indicates that 67.7% of analysed wells are showing rise and 33.0% are showing fall in water level. Remaining 0.3% stations analysed do not show any change in water level.
texts.text: Groundwater Levels :  About 43.1% of the total monitoring wells show depth to water level in the range of 0 to 5 m below ground level and 37.6% show depth to water level in the range of 5 to 10 m below ground level. About 19.2% of the total monitoring wells show water  level  deeper  than  10  m  below  ground  level.  The  groundwater  level  fluctuation analysis  of  Pre-monsoon  2025  with decadal mean  of  Pre-monsoon (2015-2024) indicates that 67.7% of analysed wells are showing rise and 33.0% are showing fall in water level. Remaining 0.3% stations analysed do not show any change in water level.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As  per  the  Ground  Water  Drought  Indices  (GWDI),  in  pre-monsoon  2025,  90%  of  the analysed  wells  are  in  the  normal  category,  while  10%  of  the  analysed  wells  are  in  the drought category.
texts.text: As  per  the  Ground  Water  Drought  Indices  (GWDI),  in  pre-monsoon  2025,  90%  of  the analysed  wells  are  in  the  normal  category,  while  10%  of  the  analysed  wells  are  in  the drought category.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against  a  requirement  of  164.05  lakh  quintals,  ensuring  a  surplus  of  14.60  lakh  quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against  a  requirement  of  164.05  lakh  quintals,  ensuring  a  surplus  of  14.60  lakh  quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests  &  Diseases: Intensity  of  pests  and  diseases,  at  some  fields,  has  been  reported  to  be above  Economic  Threshold  Level  for  Rice  (Swarming  Caterpillar)  in  Assam,  (SRBSDV)  in Haryana, and for Maize (Downey Mildew) in Karnataka. The Advisory has been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular monitoring is being carried out. The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests  &  Diseases: Intensity  of  pests  and  diseases,  at  some  fields,  has  been  reported  to  be above  Economic  Threshold  Level  for  Rice  (Swarming  Caterpillar)  in  Assam,  (SRBSDV)  in Haryana, and for Maize (Downey Mildew) in Karnataka. The Advisory has been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular monitoring is being carried out. The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. RAINFALL
texts.text: 1. RAINFALL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  During the week (31 July - 06 Aug 2025), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 24%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st June  to  06 th Aug  2025)  is  higher  than LPA by 3%.
texts.text:  During the week (31 July - 06 Aug 2025), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 24%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st June  to  06 th Aug  2025)  is  higher  than LPA by 3%.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rainfall Forecast:
texts.text: Rainfall Forecast:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Overall,  rainfall  activity  is  likely  to  be  above  normal  over  most  parts  of  Peninsular  and Central India; below normal over most parts of western Himalayan states, east and north east India and near normal over rest of the country during the week.
texts.text:  Overall,  rainfall  activity  is  likely  to  be  above  normal  over  most  parts  of  Peninsular  and Central India; below normal over most parts of western Himalayan states, east and north east India and near normal over rest of the country during the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The western end of monsoon trough at mean sea level runs north of its normal position and eastern end runs near the foothills of the Himalayas at mean sea level.
texts.text:  The western end of monsoon trough at mean sea level runs north of its normal position and eastern end runs near the foothills of the Himalayas at mean sea level.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Maximum temperatures are likely to be above normal by 2-3°C over many parts of Odisha, Chhattisgarh, Telangana, Coastal Andhra Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh, Maharashtra, Gujarat, Rajasthan, and Interior Karnataka. These are likely to be below normal or near normal over rest parts of the country.

These are likely to be below normal or near normal over rest parts of the country.

texts.text: Soil Moisture Observation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Soil moisture as on 07 August 2025 was similar/better than the average of the past 9 years in major  parts  of  Rajasthan,  M.P,  Chhattisgarh,  Jharkhand,  West  Bengal,  Haryana,  Kerala, Karnataka, Maharashtra, Telangana, Rayalseema, region of A.P. and Tamil Nadu.
texts.text:  Soil moisture as on 07 August 2025 was similar/better than the average of the past 9 years in major  parts  of  Rajasthan,  M.P,  Chhattisgarh,  Jharkhand,  West  Bengal,  Haryana,  Kerala, Karnataka, Maharashtra, Telangana, Rayalseema, region of A.P. and Tamil Nadu.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  It  was  lower than average of past 9 years in Northern parts of Bihar, North Eastern parts of U.P., Assam, in parts of  Saurashtra  region  of  Gujarat,  Southern  parts  of  Odisha  and  Coastal districts of AP.
texts.text:  It  was  lower than average of past 9 years in Northern parts of Bihar, North Eastern parts of U.P., Assam, in parts of  Saurashtra  region  of  Gujarat,  Southern  parts  of  Odisha  and  Coastal districts of AP.
